documentation.value: Declares a section header. All key-value pairs following this header belong to this section until the next section header.
Assigns a value to a key within the current section. The value extends to the end of the line.
The property value (string, number, or boolean).
A line comment. Everything after the semicolon is ignored by the parser.
An alternative line comment syntax. Everything after the hash is ignored by the parser.
Variable interpolation (supported by some INI parsers). References the value of another key.
The key whose value to substitute.
Section inheritance (supported by some INI parsers). The section inherits all key-value pairs from the parent section.
The parent section to inherit from.
Array-style value assignment (supported by some INI parsers). Multiple values can be assigned to the same key as a list.
The property name for the array.
A value to append to the array.
A quoted string value. Use quotes to preserve leading/trailing whitespace or include special characters.
The value enclosed in double quotes.
